zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas William White於1774年5月30日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可時，他的父親Thomas White38歲，母親Dorothea Brass34歲。他和Elizabeth Sisson至少有6個兒子和10個女兒。他去世於1862年3月2日在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓享年87歲，埋葬在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓霍其頓 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas William White于1774年5月30日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 布盧斯維其可时，他的父亲Thomas White38岁，母亲Dorothea Brass34岁。他和Elizabeth Sisson至少有6个儿子和10个女儿。他去世于1862年3月2日在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓享年87岁，埋葬在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可霍其頓霍其頓 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Established as a Separate Colony
historicEvents.description: In 1784, New Brunswick became a separate colony. It is the only bilingual province. The captial is Fredericton.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: First Bank
historicEvents.description: March 25, 1820, the first bank located in Saint John, New Brunswick, was established.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

historicEvents.title: Marco Polo Launches
historicEvents.description: In 1851, New Brunswick became known for being home to the world's fastest clipper ship, the Marco Polo. It was a three masted clipper ship. It's maiden voyage was to Liverpool and only took 15 days. It sailed from Liverpool to Melbourne in 76 days.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Parks Canada Directory of Federal Heritage Designations- Career of the Marco Polo (Vessel)

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas William White was born on 30 May 1774, in New Brunswick, British Colonial America, his father, Thomas White, was 38 and his mother, Dorothea Brass, was 34. He had at least 6 sons and 10 daughters with Elizabeth Sisson. He died on 2 March 1862, in Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, United States, at the age of 87, and was buried in Hodgdon Cemetery, Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas William White est né le 30 mai 1774, à Nouveau-Brunswick, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Thomas White, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Dorothea Brass, avait 34 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 10 filles avec Elizabeth Sisson. Il est décédé le 2 mars 1862, à Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterré à Hodgdon Cemetery, Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas William White am 30. Mai 1774 in New Brunswick, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas White 38 und seine Mutter Dorothea Brass 34. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Sisson mindestens 6 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Er starb am 2. März 1862 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Hodgdon Cemetery, Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas William White è nato il il 30 maggio 1774 nel Nuovo Brunswick, British Colonial America, il suo padre, Thomas White, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Dorothea Brass, aveva 34 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 10 figlie con Elizabeth Sisson. Morì il 2 marzo 1862, nel Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolto a Hodgdon Cemetery, Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas William Whiteが1774年5月30日にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ブルンスウィックで生まれた時，父Thomas Whiteは38歳，母Dorothea Brassは34歳であった。彼はElizabeth Sissonとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と10人の娘をもうけた。彼は1862年3月2日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクホードグドンで87歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクホードグドンホードグドン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas William White(이)가 1774년 5월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 브런스윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas White(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothea Brass(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Sisson(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1862년 3월 2일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 호지던에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 호지던 호지던 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas William White nasceu em 30 de maio de 1774, em Novo Brunswick, British Colonial America, seu pai, Thomas White, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Dorothea Brass, tinha 34 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 10 filhas com Elizabeth Sisson. Ele faleceu em 2 de março de 1862, em Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 87 anos, e foi sepultado em Hodgdon Cemetery, Hodgdon, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas William White родился 30 мая 1774 г. в местности Нью-Брансуик, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Thomas White было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothea Brass, 34. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 10 дочерей от Elizabeth Sisson. Он умер 2 марта 1862 г. в местности ходждон, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 87 лет и был похоронен в местности ходждон кеметири, ходждон, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.
